---
name: 13.19-create-19-workflow-call-workflow
description: Create the 19 called GitHub Actions reusable workflow that validates and translates report titles for caller workflows.
---

# Create 19 Called Workflow

## Objective

Create `.github/workflows/19-called-workflow.yml` if it has been deleted or needs to be rebuilt.

This prompt is intentionally written as a natural-language workflow request rather than a literal YAML template. Use it to demonstrate a reusable workflow that accepts inputs, validates them, emits outputs, and can be called by workflows 20 and 21.

## Natural-Language Workflow Specification

Create a GitHub Actions workflow named `19-workflow-call-workflow` in `.github/workflows/19-called-workflow.yml`.

The workflow should introduce reusable workflow definitions:

- Trigger only through `workflow_call`.
- Define required string inputs named `report_title`, `source_language`, `target_language`, and `caller_workflow`.
- Define workflow outputs named `report_title` and `translated_title`.
- Configure read-only repository contents permission for `GITHUB_TOKEN`.
- Define one job named `reusable-report` that runs on `ubuntu-latest` with a 5-minute timeout.
- Promote the `translate-report-title` step output named `translated_title` to a job output, then to the `workflow_call` output.
- Add a step named `print-reusable-report` that prints reusable workflow context in this order: enterprise, organization, repository, branch, workflow, report title, source language, and target language.
- Add a Bash step named `translate-report-title` that places the inputs in environment variables before using them.
- Enforce a `report_title` maximum length of 128 characters in the called workflow.
- Validate `source_language` and `target_language` against `en`, `fr`, `de`, and `nl-BE`.
- Return the original report title when source and target languages are the same.
- Use deterministic Bash `case` statements for report-title and target-language translation pairs.
- Write `translated_title` to `$GITHUB_OUTPUT` with multi-line output syntax.

## Translation Values

Use these deterministic translations for the reusable workflow demonstration.

For `The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog`:

- `en`: `The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog`
- `fr`: `Le vif renard brun saute par-dessus le chien paresseux`
- `de`: `Der schnelle braune Fuchs springt über den faulen Hund`
- `nl-BE`: `De snelle bruine vos springt over de luie hond`

For `Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems: Attention is All You Need, 2027`:

- `en`: `Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems: Attention is All You Need, 2027`
- `fr`: `Avancées dans les systèmes de traitement de l'information neuronale : L'attention est tout ce dont vous avez besoin, 2027`
- `de`: `Fortschritte in neuronalen Informationsverarbeitungssystemen: Aufmerksamkeit ist alles, was Sie brauchen, 2027`
- `nl-BE`: `Vooruitgang in neurale informatieverwerkingssystemen: Aandacht is alles wat u nodig hebt, 2027`

If source and target languages are different and no deterministic translation is configured for the report title plus target-language pair, fail with an actionable GitHub Actions error instead of returning an unrelated sample phrase.

## Generation Requirements

1. Generate valid GitHub Actions YAML from the natural-language specification above.
2. Preserve the workflow file path, workflow name, `workflow_call` trigger, input names, output names, read-only permission, job name, timeout, runner, and step names.
3. Keep title length validation in the called workflow only so every caller gets the same rule.
4. Keep this workflow focused on defining reusable translation behavior; do not add push, pull request, schedule, or manual triggers.
5. Do not call external translation APIs from this workflow.
6. Do not add secrets or environment secrets to this reusable workflow.

## Validation

After creating the workflow, validate `.github/workflows/19-called-workflow.yml` with `actionlint` when available.

If validation cannot reach 0 errors, create `.github/workflows/logs/workflow-prompt-errors.md` and include:

- The failing workflow path
- The validation command used
- The full validation error output
- The likely cause
- Recommended fixes

Focused validation command:

```pwsh
$actionlint = Get-Command actionlint -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if ($actionlint) {
  actionlint .github/workflows/19-called-workflow.yml
}
```
